workers.task: Verify required fields per event type

critical_findings.finding: No file locking on child-agents.jsonl. Multiple writers (launcher + commander agent + squad leads) can append concurrently without coordination. Partial writes possible on crash.
Child lifecycle events (launch_requested, launch_started, completed) are entirely LLM-delegated with zero code enforcement. Only 3 launcher-side events are deterministic.
Crashes, timeouts, SIGTERM/SIGINT leave silent gaps in the ledger. No terminal event is written when agents die unexpectedly.
swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl can diverge. No reconciliation between PID tracking, exit codes, and ledger status fields.
No formal schema or validation exists. Inconsistent identifier naming (commander_id vs child_id). Missing run_id and duration_ms fields.
Timestamp spec says ISO-8601 but does not mandate UTC Z suffix. Monitor will fail on naive ISO strings. No sequence counter for ordering concurrent events.
No status transition FSM. No validation prevents impossible transitions. Status values are inconsistent (success vs completed vs failed vs platform_refused).

recommendations: Add file locking (flock/fcntl) or single-writer per ledger file
Implement a ledger validation pass in the monitor that detects missing terminal events
Add SIGTERM trap to stampede.sh that writes finalization events to all active ledgers
Define a formal JSON schema for child-agents.jsonl with required fields per event type
Add sequence numbers to events for ordering guarantees
Enforce UTC Z suffix in timestamp spec and add code validation
Add run_id to every event for cross-run correlation
Reconcile swarm-state.json counters with child-agents.jsonl on each monitor tick
